parameters.jsCode:
// Loads the extraction system prompt (kept in a Code node so it is version-controlled with the workflow export; see ai-extraction-prompt.md for the human-readable copy)
const systemPrompt = `You are an invoice data extraction engine used inside an automated accounts-payable pipeline. You will be given the raw text extracted from a supplier PDF invoice (the extraction may have imperfect spacing or line breaks due to PDF-to-text conversion). Your job is to return ONLY a single valid JSON object with the exact schema below. No prose, no markdown fences, no explanation - JSON only.

Rules:
1. If a field cannot be found, return null for it (never omit the key).
2. Normalize all dates to ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD).
3. Normalize all monetary amounts to plain numbers using "." as the decimal separator.
4. currency must be a 3-letter ISO 4217 code, inferred from symbols/context if needed.
5. vat_percent is a number, e.g. 19 for 19%.
6. line_items is an array of { description, quantity, unit_price, line_total }, excluding blank rows or repeated headers.
7. confidence_score is your own 0-1 estimate of extraction confidence.
8. anomalies is an array of short flags for a human reviewer, e.g. mismatched totals, missing IBAN, due date before invoice date. Empty array if none.
9. Never invent values; prefer null plus an anomaly note over a guess.

Return strictly this JSON shape: {"vendor":null,"vendor_uid":null,"vendor_iban":null,"invoice_number":null,"invoice_date":null,"due_date":null,"net_amount":null,"vat_amount":null,"vat_percent":null,"gross_amount":null,"currency":null,"cost_center":null,"line_items":[],"confidence_score":0,"anomalies":[]}`;
return [{ json: { ...$json, systemPrompt } }];

value.PDF Attachment: ={{ [{ url: $node['Extract From File - PDF Text'].binary[Object.keys($node['Extract From File - PDF Text'].binary)[0]].id ? undefined : undefined }] }}

nodes.name: Airtable - Attach Original PDF
nodes.notes: n8n's Airtable node accepts binary data directly for Attachment fields when 'Attachments' column type is used with binary input mapping - configure the attachment field mapping to the incoming binary property in the UI (see README: Attachment upload note).

value.Details: ={{ 'Invoice record created via AI extraction. Confidence: ' + $node['Code - Clean & Validate AI Output'].json.confidence_score + '. Anomalies: ' + JSON.stringify($node['Code - Clean & Validate AI Output'].json.anomalies) }}

parameters.text: =:warning: Invoice extraction failed for email from *{{ $node['Set - Capture Email Metadata'].json.emailMeta.senderEmail }}* (subject: "{{ $node['Set - Capture Email Metadata'].json.emailMeta.subject }}").\nError: {{ $json.errorMessage }}\nThe email has been left in the inbox for manual handling.

value.Details: ={{ 'PDF extraction/AI parsing failed for email from ' + $node['Set - Capture Email Metadata'].json.emailMeta.senderEmail + '. Error: ' + $json.errorMessage }}
